chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now to understand this first of all we need to find what is complement means.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now when you say complement it will simply do reverse of your binary format example.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: If you say complement 1 it will give you 0 and if you say complement 0 it will give you

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: That's what it's doing.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now the same thing is applied on 12 right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And what is 12.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: 12 is actually if you convert 12 into binary format you will get this.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So you will get four zeros double 1 double 0.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: That's your 12 binary format.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And I love this mobile phone model right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Nokia double 1 double 0.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Everyone love it.

chunks.text: Now we need to find a complement of it right.

chunks.text: So you have to find the complement of it.

chunks.text: So what you will do is you will reverse the number.

chunks.text: So this 0 becomes 1 and then this is 1.

chunks.text: Then this is 0 0 1 1 1 1.

chunks.text: So this is your complement of 12.

chunks.text: But hold on.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now we have a concept of two's complement because what happens is in your system we

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: can store positive numbers right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We can store 12.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We can store 13.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We can store 15 20 21 or maybe 1000.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: But how to store negative numbers.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now to store negative numbers.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: First of all we don't store negative numbers in the system.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We always store positive numbers.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So even if you want to store this minus 13 you need to convert this number into positive

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: number first.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And the way you can do that is with the help of two's complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now how do you find two's complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So to find two's complement we need to find one's complement plus one.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: This is a formula OK.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So when you say two's complement it is one's complement plus one.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now how do you find one's complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: It's easy actually.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: First convert this number into binary format.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now what is 13.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: This is a binary format of plus 13.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So this is 13 OK.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We want to find minus 13 right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So you have to find two's complement of this number.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now let's find the one's complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And how do you find one's complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: It's easy now.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We know how to find complement right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So we have to reverse this number.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So you will get 1 1 1 1 0 0 1 0.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So this is one's complement of 13.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: But we want to find two's complement right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So what you will do you will say plus one.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now this is so when you say 0 plus 1 it will give you 1.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And then we got 1.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Then we got 0 0 1 1 1 1.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: Now this number here this is minus 13 OK.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So this is two's complement of 13 which is minus 13.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: We got it.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And now if you compare this number with complement of 12 they are same.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: And that's why you got.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So when you say complement of 12 you got minus 13 right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So that was the answer.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So that's how you can work with complement.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Python BitWise Operators
chunks.text: So when you want to store negative number this is what you do.

chunks.text: Now I would recommend you to try different numbers here.

chunks.text: So that's your complement operator.
